name: Kapildui and Butxisolo Loop
name_original: Monte Kapildui desde Arluzea — circular por Parque Natural de Izki
description: Hike the Monte Kapildui from Arluzea circular trail, a moderate 8.3-mile route through Izki Natural Park with 1392 feet of gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Bernedo, a municipality in Álava, Spain, offers access to diverse landscapes centered around the Izki Natural Park. The region is characterized by a mix of forests, open spaces, and varied terrain, providing a range of hiking experiences. Elevations include hills like Kapildui, and historical elements such as the remnants of Bernedo Castle add cultural interest to the trails. Best hiking trails around Bernedo The most popular hiking route is San Tirso from Santuario de Okón — Southern Sierras of Álava, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views across the Southern Sierras of Álava. Another top favourite among local hikers is Butxisolo and Kapildui - circular through the oak forests of Okina, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This trail leads through the oak forests of Okina, ascending to the hill of Kapildui. Local hikers also love the Kapildui and Butxisolo Loop, a 8.2 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain within the Izki Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Bernedo is defined by the expansive Izki Natural Park, ancient oak forests, and varied terrain including hills and historical castle remnants. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family trails to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Bernedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Bernedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bernedo?
A: Bernedo offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 30 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging moderate trails, ensuring options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Bernedo?
A: The terrain around Bernedo is diverse, largely due to its integration with the Izki Natural Park. You can expect a mix of ancient oak forests, open spaces, and varied landscapes, including hills like Kapildui. Some trails also feature historical elements such as the remnants of Bernedo Castle.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Bernedo?
A: Yes, Bernedo offers several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. There are 3 easy routes available, providing gentle paths through the region's natural beauty.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Bernedo?
A: While hiking in Bernedo, you can explore the expansive Izki Natural Park, known for its unique ecosystems. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of the Witch's Hut&lt;/a&gt; or the remnants of Bernedo Castle. Natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5150732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Prao de la Paul&lt;/a&gt; and various summits.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or summits worth visiting?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts several notable summits and viewpoints. Hikers can aim for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1254551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sleeping Lion Peak (Lapoblación Castle)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2921891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pagogan&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1255178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joar Summit (1,414 m)&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Bernedo?
A: Yes, there are trails suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250396433/izki-family-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Izki Family Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers an accessible and enjoyable experience for all ages within the beautiful Izki Natural Park.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bernedo?
A: The hiking trails around Bernedo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the beauty of the Izki Natural Park, and the well-maintained paths that offer a rewarding outdoor experience.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Bernedo?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Bernedo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035138157/butxisolo-and-kapildui-circular-through-the-oak-forests-of-okina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butxisolo and Kapildui - circular through the oak forests of Okina&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through scenic oak forests and up to Kapildui hill.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Bernedo?
A: Hikes in Bernedo vary in length and duration. Moderate routes, which make up the majority, can range from around 5.4 miles (8.8 km) taking about 3 hours 51 minutes, to longer trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035148016/pagogan-and-zalbizkar-from-okina-circular-through-ayuda-river-gorge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pagogan and Zalbizkar from Okina&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 10 miles (16 km) and can take around 4 hours 45 minutes.

Q: Is Bernedo a good destination for exploring natural parks?
A: Bernedo is an excellent destination for natural park exploration, as it is largely defined by its proximity to and integration with the Izki Natural Park. The park offers an expansive area with diverse landscapes, making it ideal for nature enthusiasts and providing an interpretation center for visitors.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Bernedo?
A: While specific regulations for dogs can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural parks like Izki, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. It's always recommended to check local signage or park guidelines for any specific restrictions before heading out.
tips: This is a beautiful waterfall near a cave just off the main trail. On a hot day it is a good place to cool off.
The top of Kapildui has no views, but the climb from Arluzea through the Molino ravine is beautiful.

faqs.question: How long should I expect the Monte Kapildui hike to take?
faqs.answer: The circular route from Arluzea is approximately 13.3 kilometers long and typically takes around 4 hours and 4 minutes to complete. This is an estimate, so plan for a bit more time depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This hike is classified as moderate. It requires good physical fitness and sure-footedness, especially on certain sections of the varied terrain. While not overly strenuous, it's not suitable for absolute beginners without some hiking experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the hike from Arluzea?
faqs.answer: The trail begins in Arluzea. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally advisable to look for designated parking areas within or at the edge of the village, respecting local signage and private property. Arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the Monte Kapildui trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, characteristic of Izki Natural Park. You'll find yourself on paths through extensive forests, potentially encountering rocky or uneven sections, particularly on the ascent to Monte Kapildui. The need for sure-footedness suggests a mix of surfaces, including some hilly areas within the limestone mountains.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: While the specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, Izki Natural Park generally welcomes dogs. However, it's always best practice to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons, but spring and autumn are often ideal. In spring, the forests are lush and vibrant, while autumn offers beautiful foliage. Summer can be pleasant, but always check the weather forecast for high temperatures. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for colder, potentially wet or icy conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for Parque Natural de Izki?
faqs.answer: As of current information, there are no specific permits required or entrance fees to access Parque Natural de Izki or to hike this particular trail. The park is open to the public for enjoyment, but always adhere to general park regulations regarding conservation and responsible recreation.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or natural features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the beautiful Río Molino Waterfall, especially scenic during wetter periods. The trail also leads to the Kapildui Summit, the highest point in Izki Natural Park. The ascent from Arluzea through the Molino gorge is particularly noted for its beauty, and the park itself is home to extensive Pyrenean oak forests and diverse ecosystems.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other trails within the park. You will find yourself on parts of the Postaria trail for about 17% of the route, and a smaller section of the Txarabitana trail, covering approximately 3% of the journey.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead in Arluzea?
faqs.answer: Arluzea is a small village, so amenities might be limited. It's advisable to bring your own water and snacks. While specific cafes or public restrooms directly at the trailhead are not guaranteed, you might find local establishments in Arluzea or nearby larger towns. Plan to be self-sufficient for the duration of your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I spot in Parque Natural de Izki?
faqs.answer: Izki Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including golden eagles, peregrine falcons, Egyptian vultures, and common vultures. Mammals like wild boars and foxes also inhabit the park. Respect their natural habitat and observe from a distance.
